recent_calls.summary: The user, Earl Lafferty, reported a gurgling sound from his water heater when running the washer and scheduled a service appointment for March 31st at 8:00 a.m. The agent confirmed the details and booked the appointment successfully. The user declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.

The user, Madonna White, called about a thermostat stuck on the heating setting with unresponsive buttons. The agent provided available appointment times for a technician visit but the user decided to call back later to schedule. The call ended with the user acknowledging the information and no appointment was booked during the call.

The user, Catrina, called to check on a plumbing service request for an immediate clogged drain issue. The agent confirmed the details and offered available appointment slots, but the user insisted on service today and requested to speak with a live person. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to speak to a live agent to schedule an installation and confirmed that the installation is not related to a home warranty. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Catrina, called to report a completely blocked downstairs sink that is not draining and requested service as soon as possible. The agent confirmed the details, collected contact information, and assured that the service team would reach out to confirm availability. The user provided feedback rating the experience as 4 out of 5, suggesting the agent be a real human.
The user requested to speak with someone, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The call was very brief and ended immediately after the agent's initial greeting and request for the user's ZIP code. The user hung up without responding or engaging further.
The call was initiated by the user but ended abruptly without any interaction or information exchange, as the agent requested the ZIP code for service address but received no response.

Kevin called to inquire about metal filters that were not put back into his air conditioner after a recent service. The agent confirmed the details, collected the service address, and assured Kevin that the team would review the issue and get back to him on the next business day. The call ended with Kevin acknowledging the information.

The user called to inquire about using their own bottle of Freon for a cooling service repair instead of being charged for the company's Freon. The agent collected all necessary details including job number, service address, user contact information, and noted the urgency for a follow-up. The agent assured the user that the team would review the request and contact them on the next business day.
The user called to inquire about getting a service quote. The agent offered to assist and requested the user's full name to proceed, but the call ended before the user provided it.
The call was very brief and the user hung up immediately after the agent introduced herself. No further conversation or assistance took place.
The call was very brief and did not progress beyond initial greetings. The user did not engage with the agent, and the call ended shortly after it started.
The call was very brief, with the agent introducing themselves and the user responding with a short greeting before hanging up.

The user, Dale Cobb, called to check if a technician was on the way. The agent informed him that it was after business hours and could not confirm the technician's status but assured a follow-up. The call ended abruptly before the user could finish.

The user, Derek, called to schedule a maintenance visit for his water heater, furnace, and AC. The agent confirmed the details and noted the preferred day as Wednesday, with a follow-up to confirm availability. The call ended with the user declining to provide feedback but expressing thanks.

The user, Manikandan, called to inquire about a refrigerant leak in their heating and cooling system and wanted to confirm if a technician could analyze the system with only two pounds of refrigerant. The agent documented the request and informed the user that a live agent would contact them on the next business day for further discussion. The user declined additional assistance and ended the call.

The user, Dwayne Hicks, called to schedule a routine air conditioning tune-up through his American Home Shield plan. The agent confirmed the appointment details for April 1st at 10 AM and noted no emergency or special instructions. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided neutral feedback, preferring to speak with a human representative.

The user called to inquire about an appointment for air conditioner service that was expected between 1 and 3 PM but had not yet occurred. The agent informed the user that appointments cannot be confirmed after hours but assured the team would follow up next business day. The user provided service details, address, and contact information, and requested a callback to schedule the appointment.
The call was initiated by the agent who requested the user's ZIP code to start the service process, but the user did not respond and hung up shortly after.

David called after hours to report that a scheduled heating service appointment was missed and he needs immediate assistance. The agent documented the issue and informed David that the team will follow up on the next business day since live support was unavailable. The call ended with no further requests from David.

The user, Jenny from American Home Sales, called to follow up on a work order for air conditioning service at 13101 Conway Grove Lane, St. Louis. The agent confirmed the details and the preferred callback number, and the user declined information about maintenance programs. The call ended with the user agreeing to provide feedback.

The user called to cancel a scheduled appointment for furnace repair, explaining they will have their own LLC contractors handle the issue instead. The agent confirmed the cancellation details and informed the user that Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will notify the warranty company. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

Katie Brown called to check on the status of her cooling system service appointment scheduled for 4 PM today through American Home Shield. The agent confirmed the details and assured her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her shortly. Katie declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The user called and was immediately transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.
The user, Jasmine Cole, returned a missed call and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call was brief and ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

The user called to report a heating issue that occurred after a technician visit and requested a recall appointment for early Monday morning. The agent collected all necessary details including service address, contact information, and dispatch number, and confirmed the information with the user. The user declined additional information about maintenance programs and rated the experience positively.

The user called to schedule a cooling system service appointment due to inefficient blowing. The agent confirmed the user's details, offered available slots on April 1st, 2nd, or 3rd, and booked the appointment for April 1st at 2:00 p.m. The user was satisfied with the fast and efficient service and provided positive feedback.
The user called and was immediately transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

Deanna called to follow up on a service request related to her air conditioner warranty. She wanted to know the result of her service appointment and requested to speak with a live agent. The call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about a missed call they received from the number. The agent attempted to assist by asking about the nature of the call and then transferred the user to a live agent for further help.
The user called Budget and was greeted by Sophie, the assistant, who then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 17 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.

The user, Kathy Allen, called to update an existing cooling system appointment from a repair to maintenance. The agent confirmed the details and booked the maintenance appointment for Monday, April 6, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. The call concluded with the user declining to provide feedback.

The user called to inquire about the estimated time of arrival for their appointment. The agent confirmed the user's identity and phone number, then provided the appointment details, stating that technician Devon is scheduled to arrive between 2:00 and 3:00 p.m. The user had no further questions and ended the call.
The user called customer service and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 17 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user requested to speak with a live representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to inquire about options for repairing or upgrading their cooling system compressor under a home warranty. The agent collected detailed information including the user's contact details, service address, and warranty dispatch number, and confirmed the user's preferred service days. The user provided positive feedback and was informed that the team would follow up to confirm appointment options.

The user called in response to a call about furnace repairs and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

The user called to cancel their air conditioning service appointment because the issue has already been fixed. The agent confirmed the cancellation details and offered information about maintenance programs, which the user declined. The call ended with the agent asking for feedback, which the user also declined.

The user called Budget customer service and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, confirmed the user's request and completed a successful call transfer.

The user called to verify if Budget services a specific type of cooling system unit under a home warranty. The agent collected detailed information including the service address, customer name, and contact numbers, and confirmed that a technician would follow up within the hour. The user declined additional information about maintenance programs and ended the call politely.

The user, Edith from America Home Shield, called to verify if Budget services a specific type of unit but did not have the unit details. The agent offered to assist once the user obtains the information and the call ended politely without further questions.

The user called to confirm if a technician was still scheduled to come today. The agent confirmed the appointment with technician Patrick between 2:00 and 3:30 p.m. The user had no further questions and ended the call politely.

The user called to check their appointment, which was confirmed for March 30th between 8:00 and 9:00 a.m. with technician Amory. The user requested that the technician call them after the diagnosis, and the agent assured the request would be passed on. The call ended with no further questions.
The call was very brief, with the agent attempting to start the conversation by asking for the user's service address ZIP code, but the user hung up immediately.

The user called to make an appointment for a free service related to a home warranty. The agent asked several verification questions, and the user expressed some frustration with the questioning. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user initiated a call to schedule a service related to a home warranty, but the call ended abruptly before any scheduling was completed.

The user called to inquire about the ETA of a technician for their HVAC unit. The agent confirmed the user's phone number and appointment details, but since the appointment time had passed, the call was transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

Mary called to book a heating service under her home warranty due to a seized motor. The agent confirmed her details, including the service address and home warranty dispatch number, and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact her shortly. Mary declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
